question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a connection-level authentication handshake in grpcServerConfigurer() to impersonate a known signer's connection and submit transaction authorization requests that the server accepts as legitimate?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that the NettyServerBuilder in grpcServerConfigurer() is not configured with a SecurityProtocolNegotiator or SslContext, allowing plaintext connections that carry transaction authorization data without integrity protection?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the default Netty channel options (no SO_BACKLOG limit set in grpcServerConfigurer()) to flood the server's accept queue, causing legitimate signer connections to be dropped while attacker connections are processed?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a per-connection or per-user request quota in grpcServerConfigurer() to submit an unbounded number of transaction-related gRPC calls, overwhelming the authorization pipeline and causing legitimate signer requests to be starved?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not register a gRPC interceptor to validate JWT or API-key tokens, allowing any caller to invoke RPCs that internally trigger transaction operations without presenting proof of ownership?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of mutual TLS client certificate validation in grpcServerConfigurer() to connect as an anonymous client and invoke transaction RPCs that should be restricted to verified signers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not set a maxConnectionAge or maxConnectionIdle on the NettyServerBuilder, allowing persistent connections that accumulate transaction state and eventually authorize operations without re-validating signer identity?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a gRPC server-side compression policy in grpcServerConfigurer() to send highly compressed payloads (zip bomb) that expand to consume all executor threads, preventing signer-approval transactions from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a gRPC server-side retry policy, causing the server to silently retry failed transaction calls without re-verifying signer authorization on each attempt?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of a gRPC server-side hedging policy in grpcServerConfigurer() to cause the server to execute multiple concurrent instances of the same transaction RPC, potentially authorizing duplicate operations without the signer's knowledge?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a gRPC server-side circuit breaker, allowing a cascade of failed transaction authorization calls to overwhelm the server and cause it to accept subsequent calls without proper validation?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a gRPC server-side request-size limit, allowing oversized transaction payloads that cause the server to allocate excessive memory and potentially skip authorization checks due to OOM errors?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a gRPC server-side response-size limit, allowing the server to stream unbounded transaction data back to the attacker, leaking signer-related metadata that can be used to forge authorization?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a gRPC server-side connection-level flow control, allowing the attacker to send data faster than the server can process it, causing the server to buffer transaction authorization data in memory and potentially process it out of order?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a gRPC server-side stream-level flow control, allowing the attacker to open many streaming RPCs that each consume a slot in maxConcurrentCallsPerConnection, starving legitimate signer streams?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a gRPC server-side keepalive timeout, allowing the attacker to hold connections open with minimal traffic, consuming executor threads and preventing legitimate signer-approval transactions from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a gRPC server-side keepalive permit without calls, allowing the attacker to maintain idle connections that consume resources and prevent legitimate signer connections from being established?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a gRPC server-side max-connection-age grace period, allowing the attacker to keep connections alive indefinitely and accumulate transaction state that can be replayed to authorize operations without signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a gRPC server-side max-connection-idle timeout, allowing the attacker to keep idle connections open that hold transaction locks, preventing legitimate signers from acquiring those locks?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a gRPC server-side max-connection-age, allowing the attacker to maintain long-lived connections that accumulate authorization tokens or session state, eventually gaining the ability to authorize transactions without the owner's current approval?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a gRPC server-side handshake timeout, allowing the attacker to initiate many slow TLS handshakes that consume Netty worker threads and prevent legitimate signer connections from completing?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a gRPC server-side max-header-list-size, allowing the attacker to send crafted gRPC calls with oversized metadata headers that cause the server to misparse authorization metadata and accept transactions without valid signer approval?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a gRPC server-side max-frame-size, allowing the attacker to send crafted gRPC frames that cause the server to allocate excessive memory and potentially skip authorization checks due to resource exhaustion?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a gRPC server-side max-window-size, allowing the attacker to send data faster than the server can process it, causing the server to buffer transaction authorization data in memory and potentially process it out of order?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/config/GrpcConfiguration.java -> Scope: High: Authorizing transactions without approval from signers/owners'] [Function: grpcServerConfigurer()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that grpcServerConfigurer() does not configure a gRPC server-side max-ping-strikes, allowing the attacker to send many PING frames that consume server resources and prevent legitimate signer-approval transactions from being processed?
